outlook.headline: ASIC demand stays hot as hyperscaler programs expand
outlook.summary: Custom silicon demand is still broadening, with hyperscaler AI programs driving stronger design activity, more sign-off usage, and higher pull-through in test, foundry, and IP. The latest mention trend remains elevated at 0.8x and sits slightly above the trailing average, pointing to a constructive 2026Q1 setup despite a few company-specific execution risks.

drivers.label: Hyperscaler AI/custom-chip expansion
drivers.detail: Major customer programs are scaling, supporting stronger ASIC pipelines and shipment ramps.

drivers.label: Broader ecosystem pull-through
drivers.detail: Custom silicon demand is creating more starts and higher utilization across design, manufacturing, test, and interface IP.

drivers.label: New design wins and launches
drivers.detail: Recent wins and engagements suggest custom ASIC activity is still converting into concrete opportunities.

drivers.label: Healthy compute-ramp demand
drivers.detail: ASIC demand is being cited as part of the broader AI/compute ramp rather than a one-off program.

risks: NVIDIA's view that many data centers prefer systems over semi-custom chips could limit upside for pure ASIC demand.
Program concentration and ASIC design-ramp issues at Teradyne could create lumpier near-term execution.
Broadcom flagged margin pressure on ASICs, so mix and pricing remain watch items even as demand stays hot.
